title: Sim-state hash is build-config (FP-optimization) sensitive — deterministic baselines are Release-locked; bare cmake build reddens tape/combat lanes
repro: cmake -B build (no -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE) → empty type → sim-state hash differs from Release baseline → combat_route_capture_smoke + tape_regression FAIL 'sim diverged'. FP contraction/reordering differs at -O0 vs -O2.

history.note: Both FID-0054 and FID-0058 agents hit this on fresh /tmp builds. MITIGATED (this FID): CMakeLists now defaults CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release when unset (single-config generators) so bare cmake -B build reproduces canonical hashes. DEEPER (open): the sim is not bit-reproducible ACROSS optimization levels — determinism holds WITHIN a config only. Full cross-config reproducibility would need strict-FP flags (-ffp-contract=off etc.) on the sim TUs; scoped but not done (risk: perf + may shift the canonical hash → rebaseline). Lanes also print a 'not the canonical Release build' hint. Netplay/replay/oracle baselines are config-locked until strict-FP lands.
